summary: A \boxed{\text{prospective cohort}} study should ideally be used as its strengths lie in maintaining low-bias observations by prospectively monitoring subjects' health status relative to their exposure history without significant delays or reliance purely upon past records prone to inaccuracies such as those seen with cross-sectionals or cases-controls alone under similar timelines constraints provided herein. The primary advantage lies within directly observing incidence patterns which align well towards timely assessment needs outlined initially regarding causal linkages sought after concerning public
